title: Packaged factories
description: First-party @you/* packaged factories invoked with you run --named from the you-agent-factory CLI.
openingSummary: Packaged factories are first-party built-in named factories such as @you/goal, @you/fusion, and @you/tts that you invoke with you run --named.

whatItCovers.title: What It Covers
whatItCovers.body: Packaged factories are first-party built-in named factories such as @you/goal, @you/fusion, and @you/tts. Use this surface when you need the shipped you-agent-factory terminology for choosing a packaged factory, referencing it by canonical name, understanding first-use materialization under ~/.you-agent-factory/factories, and knowing where editability and portability end.

keyConcepts.title: Key Concepts
keyConcepts.body: A packaged factory is a catalog-backed built-in under the @you/* namespace. Invoke it with you run --named <canonical-name>. Built-ins ship with the CLI catalog and materialize lazily into ~/.you-agent-factory/factories on first use. Project-local or saved named factories and portable --factory <factory.json> runs are separate invocation paths.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Run a packaged factory by its canonical name with you run --named. Pass one-shot invocation text when the factory accepts shared DEFAULT handling. Open packaged CLI topics such as you docs packaged-goal when you need per-factory invocation signatures.

limitsAndAssumptions.title: Limits And Assumptions
limitsAndAssumptions.body: This page is the web reference for first-party @you/* packaged factories. It is not a sync of packaged CLI markdown, not operator-defaults depth (see Global configuration factories), not factory.json topology (see Configuration), and not a per-factory invocation-signature deep dive. Sibling documentation links aid discovery only.

links.goalCommandLabel: Run the packaged goal factory with one-shot invocation text:

whenToChoose.body: Choose a packaged factory when you want a catalog-backed named invocation by canonical name such as @you/goal. Use a project-local or saved named factory when you own the definition under a chosen root. Use --factory <factory.json> for a portable one-shot config path without guessing --dir.

referenceAndResolution.body: Invoke with you run --named <canonical-name>. Resolution order is project-local ./factory (or --dir), then global ~/.you-agent-factory/factories, then built-in catalog materialization on first use. On-disk scoped names are URL-encoded—for example ~/.you-agent-factory/factories/@you%2Ftts/.

freshnessAndPortability.body: The built-in catalog ships with the CLI. First use materializes an editable on-disk factory; later runs load that materialized copy under the resolved root instead of overwriting customer edits. Deep per-factory invocation signatures stay on packaged CLI topics such as you docs packaged-goal, you docs packaged-fusion, and you docs packaged-tts. This page does not invent auto-refresh or package-lock semantics.

exampleNotes.body: Replace @you/goal or @you/tts with another packaged canonical name when you need that built-in. Inspect factory-defined arguments with you run --named <canonical-name> --help.
